Code fragment: public final TableField<UserAuditRecord, Long> USERID = createField(DSL.name("userid"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false), this, "用户ID");
Explanation: The 'userid' column appears to reference the UserInfo table (based on the comment '用户ID' meaning 'User ID') but lacks a foreign key constraint. Add a foreign key constraint referencing UserInfo.ID to ensure referential integrity.

Code fragment: public final TableField<UserAuditRecord, String> ENV = createField(DSL.name("env"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR(8).nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("''"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR)), this, "环境标");

Code fragment: public final TableField<UserInfoRecord, String> NAME = createField(DSL.name("name"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR(16).nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("''"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR)), this, "账户名称");

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from USER_INFO table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Specify only the required columns explicitly, e.g., select(USER_INFO.ID, USER_INFO.NAME, ...).
Using asterisk() fetches all columns from the aliased USER_INFO table, which may include unnecessary data. Explicitly list only the columns needed for the query.
Using asterisk() in the subquery fetches all columns from USER_AUDIT table, which is inefficient. Select only the columns needed for the join condition (e.g., tb.USERID).
Using select() without columns in the union fetches all columns from USER_INFO, which retrieves unnecessary data. Specify only the required columns to match the main query's projection.
Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from USER_INFO table. Explicitly list only the columns needed for the UserInfo class.

Using LIKE with a parameter value that may contain wildcards for pattern matching is inefficient for full-text search. If searching for text within strings, consider using a full-text search index; if doing exact matching, use eq() instead of like().
